summary: Comprehensive testing completed for ContentPulse Milestone 3: Per-channel insights + drill-down views. All 4 user stories (US-301, US-302, US-303, US-304) are fully functional with proper backend APIs and frontend integration.
passed_tests: US-301: Per-Channel Time Series - Daily granularity returns ~70 data points
US-301: Per-Channel Time Series - Weekly granularity returns ~10 data points
US-301: Per-Channel Time Series - Monthly granularity returns 3 data points
US-301: Invalid channel ID format returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
US-301: Non-existent channel ID returns 404 NOT_FOUND
US-301: Frontend granularity toggle (Daily/Weekly/Monthly) works correctly
US-301: Time series chart updates when granularity changes
US-302: Content Type Performance Breakdown returns sorted data by avgEngagementRate
US-302: Response includes postType, postCount, avgImpressions, avgEngagements, avgEngagementRate
US-302: Frontend displays content type table with proper sorting
US-302: Content types (video, carousel, image, text) display with colored indicators
US-303: Best Posting Times returns top 5 slots with min 2 posts per slot
US-303: dayOfWeek range 0-6 and hour range 0-23 validation works
US-303: Results sorted by avgEngagementRate descending
US-303: Frontend displays posting time slots with day names and formatted hours
US-304: Channel Comparison accepts 2+ channel IDs and returns winners object
US-304: Single channel ID returns 400 'At least 2 channel IDs required'
US-304: Non-existent channel ID returns 404 NOT_FOUND
US-304: Frontend comparison page shows metrics cards with winner badges
US-304: Side-by-side bar chart displays comparison data correctly
US-304: Channel selection/deselection works with validation hints
All backend APIs use correct date range (2026-01-01 to 2026-03-12)
All APIs return proper error responses with correct status codes and messages
Frontend navigation between Overview, Channels, and Comparison works seamlessly
Channel detail page back navigation functions properly
Data loading states and responsive design work correctly
No JavaScript errors or console warnings observed
All interactive elements have proper hover and active states
Charts and visualizations render correctly with proper data binding
